text: Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) grounds language model outputs in external knowledge retrieved at inference time, reducing hallucination.
FAISS (Facebook AI Similarity Search) is a library for efficient similarity search and clustering of dense vectors, developed by Meta AI Research.
BM25 is a bag-of-words retrieval function that ranks documents based on query terms appearing in each document using term frequency and inverse document frequency.
Hallucination in large language models refers to the generation of content that is factually incorrect or not grounded in the provided context.
Chain-of-thought prompting encourages language models to generate intermediate reasoning steps before producing a final answer, improving accuracy on complex tasks.
Natural Language Inference (NLI) is the task of determining whether a hypothesis is entailed by, contradicted by, or neutral with respect to a given premise.
Cross-encoders jointly encode a query-document pair to produce a relevance score, yielding higher accuracy than bi-encoders but at greater computational cost.
Reciprocal Rank Fusion (RRF) combines ranked lists from multiple retrieval systems by assigning scores based on inverse rank position of each document.
Qwen is a series of large language models developed by Alibaba Cloud, available in multiple sizes and capable of instruction following and reasoning.
Ollama is a tool that allows users to run large language models locally on their own hardware with a simple command-line interface.
Sentence transformers produce meaningful sentence embeddings for semantic similarity tasks by fine-tuning transformer models on sentence pairs.
RAGAS is an evaluation framework for Retrieval-Augmented Generation systems measuring faithfulness, answer relevancy, context precision, and context recall.
DeBERTa improves BERT using disentangled attention and an enhanced mask decoder, achieving strong performance on NLI and other NLP benchmarks.
Adaptive RAG systems dynamically adjust retrieval strategy based on query complexity, using simpler strategies for easy queries and multi-step retrieval for hard ones.
Self-RAG trains language models to generate reflection tokens that control when and how to retrieve, allowing the model to critique its own outputs.
The Monsoon HPC cluster at Northern Arizona University provides A100 GPU resources for large-scale machine learning research and experimentation.
Prompt engineering involves crafting input prompts to guide language model behavior without updating model weights, using in-context learning techniques.
Vector databases store high-dimensional embeddings and support efficient approximate nearest-neighbor search, enabling semantic retrieval at scale.
LoRA (Low-Rank Adaptation) is a parameter-efficient fine-tuning technique that adds trainable low-rank matrices to frozen pre-trained model weights.
Quantization reduces model size and inference cost by representing weights with lower precision such as INT8 or INT4 instead of FP32.
TriviaQA is a reading comprehension dataset containing trivia questions paired with evidence documents from Wikipedia and the web.
Natural Questions is a dataset of questions issued to the Google search engine paired with Wikipedia articles containing the answers.
Faithfulness in RAG evaluation measures whether the generated answer is factually consistent with the retrieved context provided to the model.
TruLens is an evaluation library for LLM applications that provides feedback functions to measure hallucination rate, relevance, and groundedness.
Ablation studies systematically remove components from a model to measure the individual contribution of each part to overall system performance.
HotpotQA is a question answering dataset requiring multi-hop reasoning across multiple documents to arrive at the correct answer.
The attention mechanism in transformers computes a weighted sum of value vectors where weights are determined by compatibility between queries and keys.
PyTorch is an open-source machine learning framework developed by Meta AI, widely used for deep learning research and production deployments.
Cosine similarity measures the cosine of the angle between two vectors and is commonly used to compare document embeddings in information retrieval.
Confidence calibration ensures that a model's predicted confidence scores accurately reflect the true probability of the answer being correct.
IEEE Access is a multidisciplinary open-access journal publishing peer-reviewed research across all IEEE fields of interest with rapid publication times.
Knowledge distillation is a compression technique where a smaller student model is trained to mimic the behavior of a larger teacher model.
Perplexity measures how well a probability model predicts a sample. Lower perplexity means the model is more confident in its predictions.
Agentic AI systems use language models as reasoning engines that can plan multi-step actions, use tools, and adapt based on environmental feedback.
Grounding in AI connects model outputs to real-world facts or external documents rather than relying solely on the model's parametric memory.
The BEIR benchmark is a heterogeneous retrieval benchmark covering 18 diverse datasets for evaluating zero-shot information retrieval models.
Sparse retrieval methods like BM25 rely on exact lexical matching and are fast, interpretable, and effective for keyword-heavy queries.
Dense retrieval uses neural encoders to represent queries and documents as vectors, enabling semantic matching beyond simple keyword overlap.
Multi-hop reasoning requires synthesizing information from multiple documents or performing multiple reasoning steps to answer a single question.
Open-domain question answering requires answering questions without a predefined candidate set, needing broad retrieval over large document corpora.
LangChain is a framework for building applications with large language models, providing tools for chaining prompts, memory management, and agents.
Embeddings are dense vector representations of text that capture semantic meaning and enable mathematical operations on natural language.
SLURM is a workload manager used by HPC clusters to schedule and manage computational jobs across multiple nodes and GPU resources.
Hugging Face is a platform providing open-source NLP tools, model hosting, and datasets widely used by the machine learning research community.
Transformer models use self-attention mechanisms to process sequences of data. BERT, GPT, and T5 are all well-known transformer-based architectures.
Few-shot learning allows models to generalize from a small number of examples provided in the prompt context without any gradient updates.
Named Entity Recognition is the task of locating and classifying named entities in text into categories such as person, organization, or location.
Machine learning is a subset of artificial intelligence that gives systems the ability to learn and improve from experience without being explicitly programmed.
The Python programming language emphasizes code readability and simplicity and has become the dominant language for machine learning and data science.
Retrieval systems combine dense and sparse approaches using score fusion techniques like Reciprocal Rank Fusion to leverage the strengths of both methods.

E Access Target  Tech: PyTorch, YOLO11m, Swin Transformer, FastAPI, React.js, SLURM, A100 GPUs, OpenCV  • Trained custom YOLO11m object detection models on NAU’s Monsoon HPC cluster (A100 GPUs) to classify 7 vehicle damage types across the  VehiDE dataset, achieving mAP@0.5 of 0.532 with targeted benchmark of 0.620+.  • Designed a hybrid two-stage pipeline: YOLO11m for localization + fine-tuned Swin Transformer for classification, achieving 88.8% validation  accuracy on damage crop classificatio

validation  accuracy on damage crop classification.  • Engineered custom training pipeline with weighted random sampling, class-specific loss adjustments, cosine LR scheduling, and AMP debugging  (resolved NaN gradient issues) to handle severe class imbalance.  • Shipped a full-stack AI web application (FastAPI + React) with drag-and-drop image upload, real-time inference, bounding-box overlays, and  confidence score visualization. ng Adaptive RAG System  EMNLP/NAACL Workshop Target  Tech: Python, FAISS, BM25, DeBERTa-v3, Qwen/Llama-3-8B, LangChain, Cross-Encoder, HPC  • Architected a closed-loop RAG system with hybrid retrieval (FAISS + BM25 + Reciprocal Rank Fusion), cross-encoder re-ranking, and NLI-based  self-verification (DeBERTa-v3) that automatically detects and corrects hallucinated or unsupported claims.  • Built a Super-Intelligence orchestration layer that dynamically selects retrieval strategies, adjusts chunk

ically selects retrieval strategies, adjusts chunk sizes, and triggers agentic retry loops  based on critic feedback – improving retrieval precision over single-pass baselines.  • Integrated Qwen/Llama-3-8B for generation via Ollama/vLLM serving, with structured prompt templates and function-calling patterns for tool- augmented responses.
